pattern: The result is just the leading chunk read backwards glued to whatever wasn't touched, so this is a slicing-and-reversal exercise rather than anything algorithmic.
hints: Split the string into two pieces: the part that gets reversed and the part that's left alone.
Reverse only the first piece, then stick the untouched remainder back on after it.

Watch the boundary carefully: the reversed piece is exactly the first k characters, and the remainder starts right after that -- nothing from the remainder should slip into the reversal or vice versa.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, k equals the full length
statement example, k=1 leaves the string visually unchanged
k equals the full length of a longer string
k lands in the middle, remainder must be preserved untouched and in order
repeated characters make an incorrect off-by-one reversal easy to miss

stress.description: random lowercase strings up to the constraint's max length with a random valid k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    length = rng.randint(1, n)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(length))
    k = rng.randint(1, length)
    return [s, k]
